gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: TWAP manipulation via oldestObservation fallback and fixed 10% slippage allows attacker to drain protocol value in swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn
candidate.claimed_root_cause: getTorusQuoteForTitanX uses a TWAP with an oldestObservation fallback that collapses the window to near-spot for low-cardinality pools; amountOutMinimum is derived solely from TWAP * 90%, with no spot-vs-TWAP divergence check; swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn is permissionless, letting the attacker time the call after manipulating the TWAP.
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker manipulates the TitanX/TORUS pool TWAP downward, triggers the protocol swap at a deflated amountOutMinimum, protocol burns fewer TORUS per TitanX spent, attacker profits from the price spread by buying TORUS back cheaply after the protocol swap restores price.

live_preconditions.name: titanXIntervalsBurn[lastTitanXBurnIntervalNumber].amountAllocated > 0 and amountBurned == 0
live_preconditions.observed_value: lastTitanXBurnIntervalNumber=53087; amountAllocated for interval 53087 not captured in live_context (only intervals 0 and 1 sampled, both zero)

live_preconditions.name: TitanX/TORUS pool oldestObservation (observation cardinality)
live_preconditions.observed_value: not captured; pool started ~July 2025 (startTimeStamp=1752170400), so pool is ~1 year old; oldestObservation likely >> 900s, meaning full 15-min TWAP applies and oldestObservation fallback is NOT the primary attack vector at current state
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: cast call <titanXTorusPool> 'slot0()' --rpc-url mainnet; cast call <OracleLibrary> 'getOldestObservationSecondsAgo(address)(uint32)' <titanXTorusPool> --rpc-url mainnet

live_preconditions.name: TitanX/TORUS pool liquidity depth (cost to move TWAP by >10% over 15 minutes)
live_preconditions.observed_value: not captured; INITIAL_TITAN_X_FOR_LIQ=200_000_000_000e18, INITIAL_LP_MINT=20_000e18; current depth unknown

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: TWAP oracle read with oldestObservation fallback: if oldestObservation < 900, secondsAgo is reduced to oldestObservation, collapsing the TWAP window toward spot price and making it cheaper to manipulate.

amountOutMinimum = TWAP_quote * (100 - titanXToTorusSlippage) / 100 = TWAP * 90%. No spot-vs-TWAP divergence check. If TWAP is manipulated down by >10%, the swap passes with protocol receiving far fewer TORUS.
Permissionless external function. Attacker controls timing relative to their TWAP manipulation. Incentive fee (INCENTIVE_FEE=150, BPS_DENOM=10000 => 1.5% of amountAllocated) paid to caller in TitanX.
INCENTIVE_FEE=150, BPS_DENOM=10000 (1.5% incentive to caller); POOL_FEE=10000 (1% Uniswap pool). These determine the economic parameters of the attack.

attacker_path.actor: Any unprivileged EOA or contract with sufficient TitanX and TORUS capital (or flash-loan access)
attacker_inputs: Timing of large TORUS->TitanX swap to depress pool spot price
Duration to hold position (up to 900s for full TWAP window, or oldestObservation if shorter)
call_sequence: 1. Attacker executes large TORUS->TitanX swap on TitanX/TORUS Uniswap V3 pool, depressing TORUS spot price
2. Attacker waits secondsAgo seconds (min(oldestObservation, 900)) for TWAP to accumulate the depressed price
4. Inside _swapTitanXForTorus: getTorusQuoteForTitanX returns deflated quote; amountOutMinimum = deflated * 90%
5. Uniswap router executes swap at depressed spot price; protocol receives fewer TORUS than fair value; swap passes amountOutMinimum check
6. Protocol burns the reduced TORUS amount; attacker receives 1.5% of amountAllocated as TitanX incentive
7. Attacker reverses position: buys TORUS back at still-depressed price (or price partially recovered by protocol swap), capturing the spread
state_before: Pool spot price at fair value before attacker's initial swap
state_after: Protocol burned fewer TORUS than fair-value swap would have produced
Attacker net positive: sold TORUS high, bought back low, received incentive fee

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: TitanX (protocol-held, from titanXIntervalsBurn allocation) and TORUS market value
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Price spread from selling TORUS at fair value before manipulation and buying back at depressed price after protocol swap; plus 1.5% TitanX incentive fee
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol's TitanX allocation is used to purchase fewer TORUS than fair value; fewer TORUS are burned, reducing deflationary pressure; the difference in TORUS value accrues to the attacker via the price spread
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker is entitled only to the 1.5% TitanX incentive fee for calling swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn. The additional profit from the price manipulation spread is not an entitled gain — it is extracted by exploiting the TWAP-based slippage floor and the permissionless timing of the swap.

setup: Fork Ethereum mainnet at block 25530134 (live_context captured_at block)
Query OracleLibrary.getOldestObservationSecondsAgo(pool) to get actual secondsAgo used
Query titanXIntervalsBurn(53087) to confirm amountAllocated > 0 and amountBurned == 0; if not, find the next unfilled interval
Deal attacker a large TORUS balance sufficient to move pool price by >10% (or use flash loan from pool)
Record attacker TORUS and TitanX balances before attack
transaction_sequence: attacker.swap(TORUS -> TitanX, largeAmount) on titanXTorusPool to depress spot price
vm.warp(block.timestamp + secondsAgo) to let TWAP accumulate manipulated price
attacker.swap(TitanX -> TORUS, available TitanX) to reverse position at depressed price
Compute attackerProfit = (attacker final TORUS - attacker initial TORUS) in USD-equivalent terms
expected_assertions: assertLt(torusReceived, fairQuote * 90 / 100, 'protocol received less than 90% of fair value')
assertGt(attackerProfit, 0, 'attacker net positive after gas and swap costs')
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If torusReceived >= fairQuote * 90 / 100 (pool too deep to move TWAP by >10% with realistic capital) or attackerProfit <= 0 (manipulation cost exceeds spread), downgrade to REJECT. The mature pool (~1 year old, oldestObservation >> 900s) means the full 15-minute TWAP applies, significantly raising the capital cost of manipulation compared to a new pool scenario.

rejection_reason: not_rejected: all hard gates pass except not_external_dependency_only is false because the attack profitability depends on actual Uniswap V3 pool liquidity depth and observation cardinality at the pinned block, which are external state not captured in live_context. The code path and theoretical attack are confirmed; local fork proof is required to determine whether the attack is economically viable given the mature pool state (~1 year old, full 15-min TWAP likely applies, raising manipulation cost substantially vs. the low-cardinality scenario described in the report).
